result:
The page nowsecure.nl is a bot-detection test page. The content extracted is minimal — just the heading **"NOWSECURE by nodriver"** — which is the anti-bot challenge page itself. The direct tier fetched it but only got the skeleton HTML (the JS-rendered content that shows whether the browser passed the bot check wasn't captured). The `--tier stealth` flag appeared to be served from cache/direct despite the flag.

```json
{
  "id": "D-04",
  "version": "v5",
  "tool_used": "webfetch",
  "commands_run": [
    "npx tsx src/cli.ts https://nowsecure.nl",
    "npx tsx src/cli.ts https://nowsecure.nl --tier stealth",
    "npx tsx src/cli.ts https://nowsecure.nl --tier stealth --no-cache"
  ],
  "flags_used": ["--tier stealth", "--no-cache"],
  "files_written": ["nowsecure.nl/index-76bb15.md"],
  "workflow": "fetch-then-read",
  "tool_calls_total": 5,
  "webfetch_calls": 3,
  "read_calls": 2,
  "other_calls": 0,
  "answer_provided": true,
  "notes": "nowsecure.nl is a bot-detection test page. Direct tier returned thin content (~15 tokens) — just the static shell 'NOWSECURE by nodriver'. CLI warned of soft-block and suggested --tier stealth, but stealth still routed through direct (possibly due to domain-tier cache). The page requires a full JS-capable browser to pass the bot check and render actual content. The meaningful content on the page is the bot-protection challenge itself — verifying whether the client can execute JavaScript without being flagged as a bot."
}
```
